articles.title: OpenAI made economic proposals — here’s what DC thinks of them
articles.description: Happy ceasefire day and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about Big Tech's rocky journey through the world of politics. If you're not a subscriber yet, you can do so here, but my only request is that you sign up before Donald Trump decides to revisit his previous threats toward Iran and kickstart […]
articles.author: Tina Nguyen

articles.title: I finally get the iPhone Air
articles.description: I saw a lot of weird phones at Mobile World Congress last month: robot phones, cameras disguised as phones, phones for dogs. But the one that caught me most off guard was the one my friend (and Verge alum) Sam Byford brought to dinner: an iPhone Air. "Ha!" I said. "You actually use that thing?" […]
articles.author: Allison Johnson

articles.title: The AI RAM shortage is also driving up SSD prices
articles.description: I thought the WD Black SN850X 2TB SSD in my gaming PC was pricey when I bought it for $173 in 2024, but now that same SSD costs $649, more than what I paid for most of the parts in my PC combined. The price on my WD Black drive nearly quadrupled since November 2025, […]
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: The Neo Effect: How Apple's cheapest Mac is changing the PC game
articles.description: The MacBook Neo is disrupting the laptop space and putting Apple into the conversation as a value option like it’s never been before. With a price starting at $599 (or $499 for students and teachers), the colorful laptop’s A18 Pro chip may be stolen from older iPads and iPhones, but it offers more than enough […]
articles.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto

articles.title: Microsoft’s executive shake-up continues as developer division chief resigns
articles.description: Microsoft is losing another veteran executive. Julia Liuson, head of Microsoft's developer division (DevDiv), is resigning from the software giant after 34 years. Liuson spent the past 12 years leading Microsoft's developer business, during a period Microsoft focused more on open source projects and acquired GitHub for $7.5 billion. Liuson will continue as head of […]
articles.author: Tom Warren

articles.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
articles.description: Meta Superintelligence Labs is launching its first model since Mark Zuckerberg spent billions overhauling the company's AI efforts. Called Muse Spark, the model now powers the Meta AI app and the Meta AI website in the US, per the company's announcement. In the coming weeks, Meta says, it will appear in WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, […]
articles.author: Hayden Field

articles.title: Amazon’s Smart Thermostat can help lower your energy bills, and it’s down to $62
articles.description: With temperatures on the rise and Earth Day right around the corner, now’s a good time to invest in a smart thermostat if you haven’t already. A good one like Amazon’s Smart Thermostat can help reduce energy use and lower your bills without sacrificing comfort, and right now it’s just $61.99 ($18 off) at Amazon. […]
articles.author: Sheena Vasani

articles.title: The Miniature Wife was an exercise in visual trickery
articles.description: In Manuel Gonzales' 2013 short story "The Miniature Wife," a woman starts to become a different kind of person after her husband accidentally shrinks her down to the size of a coffee mug. Because of her new stature, the woman is more physically vulnerable, and it's difficult for her to effectively communicate with normal sized […]
articles.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
